architecture_fit.note: Minimal hand-built band-argmax readout reading the exact geometry the five modes are defined by; no MLP, no training, perfect canonical accuracy.

baseline_comparison.note: Clear strawman (absolute-distance prototype match) ties on clean data but collapses at high noise, plus the 0.2 random reference — the testable contrast is shown.

faithfulness.note: Transparent hand-set rule is self-evident, but it is purely synthetic with no causal/ablation check, and the strawman is described inconsistently (L2 distance vs '3 bands only') across code/README/app.

operating_range.note: Full canonical noise sweep [0.0-0.5] covered and robustness=1.0, but the full rule never breaks in-range and they never push noise far enough to find its TAU breaking point.

hardcoded_weights_bonus.note: Entirely hand-set: TAU=0.18 threshold and closed-form prototypes, no learning — a clean hand-written circuit reproducing the target behaviour.

visual_judgement.note: Demo shows heatmap, band-mass bars with the TAU decision line, side-by-side full/strawman probabilities, and the headline accuracy-vs-noise curve — the claim is legible.

visualisation_rationale.note: 'Why this visualisation' ties the robustness curve to the headline metric and justifies axes, baseline, and the scale-invariance claim it tests.

notes: A clean, minimal hand-built classifier that achieves perfect canonical accuracy and perfect mode_robustness via a scale-invariant band-argmax-vs-threshold rule, with a strong strawman contrast and a legible, well-justified visualisation. Weaker on faithfulness — purely synthetic with no causal check and an inconsistently-described strawman — and on operating range, since the full rule never breaks within the swept noise and its breaking point is never probed. Mean of scored items ~4.4 places it solidly in 'good'.
